back_translation: A Miktam of David. Preserve me, O God, for in You I take refuge.
I say to the LORD, 'You are my Lord; apart from You I have no good.'
As for the holy ones who are in the land, they are the noble ones in whom is all my delight.
Sorrows will multiply for those who run after another god. I will not pour out their drink offerings of blood, nor take their names upon my lips.
The LORD is my portion and my cup; You hold my lot secure.
The boundary lines have fallen for me in pleasant places; surely my inheritance is beautiful to me.
I will bless the LORD who counsels me; even at night my inmost being instructs me.
I have set the LORD always before me; because He is at my right hand, I will not be shaken.
Therefore my heart is glad and my soul rejoices; my body also will dwell securely.
For You will not abandon my soul to the realm of the dead, nor will You let Your faithful one see the pit.
You will make known to me the path of life; in Your presence is fullness of joy; at Your right hand are pleasures forever.
